root_text.sn22.24:1.2: “Rūpaṁ, bhikkhave, anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya;
root_text.sn22.24:1.3: vedanaṁ anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya;
root_text.sn22.24:1.5: saṅkhāre anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya;
root_text.sn22.24:1.6: viññāṇaṁ anabhijānaṁ aparijānaṁ avirājayaṁ appajahaṁ abhabbo dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn22.24:1.7: Rūpañca kho, bhikkhave, abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāya;
root_text.sn22.24:1.11: viññāṇaṁ abhijānaṁ parijānaṁ virājayaṁ pajahaṁ bhabbo dukkhakkhayāyā”ti.

translation_text.sn22.24:1.2: “Mendicants, without directly knowing and completely understanding form, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn22.24:1.3: Without directly knowing and completely understanding feeling …
translation_text.sn22.24:1.6: consciousness, without dispassion for it and giving it up, you can’t end suffering.
translation_text.sn22.24:1.7: By directly knowing and completely understanding form, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.
translation_text.sn22.24:1.8: By directly knowing and completely understanding feeling …
translation_text.sn22.24:1.11: consciousness, having dispassion for it and giving it up, you can end suffering.”
